title: Royel Otis: meet me in the car tour
blurb: The Australian indie-rock duo brings its dreamy, surf-tinged guitar hooks to the Bowl, with Ax and the Hatchetmen opening.
address: Santa Barbara Bowl, 1122 N. Milpas Street, Santa Barbara, CA 93103
photo_query: Santa Barbara Bowl Santa Barbara
local_note: Gates open at 5:30 — give yourself about 90 minutes; Bowl parking is limited and uphill, with the Santa Barbara High lot ($20, cash) the main bet.

title: Gabriel ‘Fluffy’ Iglesias
blurb: Don’t worry, be fluffy — the Hawaiian-shirted storyteller brings his high-energy, family-friendly stand-up to the Bowl for a summer night under the oaks.
address: Santa Barbara Bowl, 1122 N. Milpas Street, Santa Barbara, CA 93103
photo_query: Santa Barbara Bowl Santa Barbara
local_note: Gates at 5:30; arrive early — Bowl parking is limited and uphill, and the Santa Barbara High lot is the go-to.

title: Young The Giant: Victory Garden Tour
blurb: The SoCal alt-rock favorites headline with Cold War Kids and Kennyhoopla — a stacked bill for a summer evening at the Bowl.
address: Santa Barbara Bowl, 1122 N. Milpas Street, Santa Barbara, CA 93103
photo_query: Santa Barbara Bowl Santa Barbara
local_note: Gates open at 5:00 for this one; plan ~90 minutes for parking and the climb up to your seat.

title: Rainbow Kitten Surprise: bones North American Tour
blurb: Rainbow Kitten Surprise rolls through with Spacey Jane — genre-bending indie to close out a July weekend at the Bowl.
address: Santa Barbara Bowl, 1122 N. Milpas Street, Santa Barbara, CA 93103
photo_query: Santa Barbara Bowl Santa Barbara
local_note: Gates at 5:30; the Santa Barbara High lot ($20, cash) two blocks down is the easiest park.

title: California Wine Festival: Sunset Rare & Reserve Tasting
blurb: The festival’s most intimate evening: a sparkling-wine reception under the stars at the Hilton’s Plaza del Sol, with rare reserve pours matched to chef bites.
address: Hilton Santa Barbara Beachfront Resort, Plaza Del Sol, 633 E Cabrillo Blvd, Santa Barbara, CA 93103
photo_query: Hilton Santa Barbara Beachfront Resort Santa Barbara
local_note: A 21+ evening; your ticket covers unlimited wine and food samples plus a keepsake glass — buy ahead, a sellout is expected.

title: California Wine Festival: Beachside Wine Festival
blurb: The signature beachside bash returns to Chase Palm Park — hundreds of California wines, craft brews, and a tri-tip throwdown, all to a live band by the sea.
address: Chase Palm Park Field – Oceanside, 236 E. Cabrillo Blvd., Santa Barbara, CA 93103
photo_query: Chase Palm Park Field – Oceanside Santa Barbara
local_note: 21+, beachside and outdoors; VIP and early entry start at noon, general at 1. Advance tickets run $10–$20 under the gate.

title: Santa Barbara Fourth of July Celebration
blurb: The city’s biggest free party of the summer — a full day of live music on West Beach capped by a 20-minute fireworks show over the Pacific.
address: West Beach, 99 W. Cabrillo Blvd., Santa Barbara, CA 93101
photo_query: West Beach Santa Barbara
local_note: Free and all-ages; you can stake out blankets and chairs from 6 a.m., and fireworks launch off West Beach around 9 p.m.

title: American Revolution Experience: A Traveling Exhibit
blurb: A free traveling exhibit at the Central Library marking America’s 250th, tracing Santa Barbara’s own threads to the Revolutionary War.
address: Santa Barbara Central Library, Fireplace Room, 40 E. Anapamu St., Santa Barbara, CA 93101
photo_query: Santa Barbara Central Library Santa Barbara
local_note: Free, during regular library hours through July 17; DAR docents may greet you to share Santa Barbara’s Revolutionary-War ties.

title: Downtown Santa Barbara Saturday Farmers’ Market
blurb: Santa Barbara’s flagship Saturday market fills State and Carrillo with local growers, flowers, and buskers — the city’s freshest morning ritual.
address: State & Carrillo Streets (Carrillo St. between Chapala & Anacapa; State St. between Canon Perdido & Figueroa), Santa Barbara, CA 93101
photo_query: State & Carrillo Streets (Carrillo St. between Chapala & Anacapa; State St. between Canon Perdido & Figueroa) Santa Barbara
local_note: Rain or shine, 8 to 1; arrive early for eggs and the best berries, and grab parking on Anacapa, Haley, or Ortega.

title: French Wave: Contempt (Le Mépris)
blurb: A French writer’s marriage frays while he doctors a film of The Odyssey for a brash American producer — screening in SBIFF’s French Wave at the Riviera.
address: SBIFF Riviera Theatre, 2044 Alameda Padre Serra, Santa Barbara, CA 93103
photo_query: SBIFF Riviera Theatre Santa Barbara
local_note: Part of SBIFF’s two-week French Wave at the historic Riviera Theatre, up on the Riviera near the El Encanto.

title: French Wave: Six Days in Spring

photo_query: SBIFF Riviera Theatre Santa Barbara
local_note: A French Wave screening at SBIFF’s year-round Riviera home; the festival runs two weeks of new French cinema.

title: Jackalope Indie Artisan Fair (Day 1)

title: Jackalope Indie Artisan Fair (Day 2)

title: Summer Concert Series: The Rincons
blurb: The Carpinteria Arts Center's free Saturday summer concert brings local favorites The Rincons to downtown Linden Avenue for an all-ages evening of music.
address: Lynda Fairly Carpinteria Arts Center, 865 Linden Ave, Carpinteria, CA 93013
photo_query: Carpinteria Arts Center Linden Avenue
local_note: Free, all ages, 6–9 p.m. on Linden; snacks and drinks for sale benefit the Arts Center. About 12 miles down the 101 from Santa Barbara.

title: Carpinteria Certified Farmers' Market
blurb: A laid-back, intimate Thursday-afternoon market on Linden Avenue with 20–30 local growers — avocados, citrus, flowers, and prepared foods, eight blocks up from the beach.
reason_to_go: Stroll the 800 block of Linden for just-picked Carpinteria-valley produce, cut flowers, and a small-town beach-town vibe.
address: 800 block of Linden Avenue (between 8th & 9th), Carpinteria, CA 93013
photo_query: Carpinteria farmers market Linden Avenue
local_note: Year-round, rain or shine, Thursday afternoons. Accepts CalFresh EBT, WIC, and cards. FLAG: end time varies by source (Visit SB 6 p.m.; some vendor pages 5:30 p.m.).

title: Island Brewing Company
blurb: A family-run, award-winning microbrewery tucked beside the railroad tracks near Carpinteria's beach and train station.
reason_to_go: Grab a flight of creative, affordable craft beers on the patio at sunset, often with live music and a food truck out back.
address: 5049 6th St, Carpinteria, CA 93013
photo_query: Island Brewing Company Carpinteria
local_note: Down a short railroad corridor near the Carpinteria train station; kid- and dog-friendly, with a food truck on the back patio.

title: Rincon Brewery
blurb: A popular Carpinteria brewpub with a varied, well-loved menu and a patio for outside dining.
reason_to_go: Pair house beers with standout wings, smash burgers, and a strong weekday happy hour.
address: 5065 Carpinteria Ave, Carpinteria, CA 93013
photo_query: Rincon Brewery Carpinteria
local_note: Industrial-upscale brewpub with an outdoor patio; Mon–Thu happy hour. (Note: this is the Carpinteria brewpub, not a Funk Zone venue.)

title: Third Window Brewing Carpinteria
blurb: A modern, well-designed brewery and kitchen on Linden with indoor seating, a fireplace, and an upstairs view deck.
reason_to_go: Settle in for hyped house beers and one of the area's best cheeseburgers, indoors by the fire or up on the deck.
address: 720 Linden Ave, Carpinteria, CA 93013
photo_query: Third Window Brewing Carpinteria
local_note: Kid- and pet-friendly; indoor and outdoor seating plus an upstairs view space. Initial service line can be long.

title: Mesa Cafe & Bar
blurb: A friendly, long-running neighborhood spot on Cliff Drive serving all-day breakfast, lunch, and inexpensive drinks.
reason_to_go: Settle in for a Santa Barbara omelette or classic Benedict — lunch served anytime — in a cheerful Mesa local's haunt.
address: 1972 Cliff Dr, Santa Barbara, CA 93109
photo_query: Mesa Cafe Bar Cliff Drive Santa Barbara
local_note: A Mesa staple known for warm service and regulars; breakfast and lunch only (kitchen closes mid-afternoon).

title: Lighthouse Coffee (Mesa)
blurb: A bright, modern Mesa coffee shop with in-house roasting, generous seating, and a cozy patio.
reason_to_go: Linger over a well-pulled latte and an almond croissant with plenty of room to work or read.
address: 1819 Cliff Dr C, Santa Barbara, CA 93109
photo_query: Lighthouse Coffee Mesa Cliff Drive Santa Barbara
local_note: Open 6 a.m.–4 p.m. daily; ample seating and parking, popular with remote workers.

title: Boathouse at Hendry's Beach
blurb: A beloved beachfront seafood restaurant right on the sand at Hendry's Beach (Arroyo Burro), with patio heaters and ocean views.
reason_to_go: Book a sunset table on the sand for fresh seafood and cocktails with your feet practically in the Pacific.
address: 2981 Cliff Dr, Santa Barbara, CA 93109
photo_query: Boathouse Hendrys Beach Santa Barbara
local_note: On the sand at Arroyo Burro / Hendry's Beach; reservations recommended, especially for sunset. Dog-friendly beach next door.

title: Lure Fish House
blurb: A well-regarded seafood restaurant at La Cumbre Plaza on upper State, known for sustainable fish, oysters, and complimentary hot sourdough.
reason_to_go: Come for fresh, sustainable seafood and charbroiled oysters — and the hot sourdough that regulars come back for.
address: 3815 State St Suite G131, Santa Barbara, CA 93105
photo_query: Lure Fish House upper State Santa Barbara
local_note: In La Cumbre Plaza on upper State; lively bar with a good happy-hour menu, plus a quieter patio.

title: Stearns Wharf
blurb: California's oldest working wooden wharf, jutting into the harbor with restaurants, the Sea Center, and sweeping mountain-and-ocean views.
reason_to_go: Stroll the historic planks for 360-degree views, touch-pool sea life at the Sea Center, and an easy sunset walk over the water.
address: 217 Stearns Wharf, Santa Barbara, CA 93101
photo_query: Stearns Wharf Santa Barbara
local_note: Open 24 hours; drive on for 90 minutes free parking, but it's easier to park and walk. The Sea Center touch pools are a kid favorite.

title: Santa Barbara Botanic Garden
blurb: Seventy-eight acres of California native plants — redwoods, meadows, and a creek — climbing the foothills above Mission Canyon with ocean views.
reason_to_go: Wander shaded native-plant trails past a stone-bridged waterfall and a redwood grove, with sweeping views from the top.
address: 1212 Mission Canyon Rd, Santa Barbara, CA 93105
photo_query: Santa Barbara Botanic Garden Mission Canyon
local_note: Open 10–5 daily; gentle slopes with some stairs. A gift shop and native-plant nursery on site. Admission charged at the gate.

title: Douglas Family Preserve
blurb: A 70-acre blufftop preserve above Arroyo Burro Beach, with mostly off-leash trails through cypress and grassland to dramatic ocean overlooks.
reason_to_go: Walk the cliff-edge loop at sunset with the dogs off-leash and the Pacific spread out below.
address: Linda Rd & Selrose Ln, Santa Barbara, CA 93109
photo_query: Douglas Family Preserve Santa Barbara
local_note: Park at the neighborhood trailhead (Linda/Selrose); the highway-side entrance has no parking. Largely off-leash for dogs.

title: Shoreline Park
blurb: A long grassy clifftop park on the Mesa with a paved oceanfront path, a playground, picnic tables, and steps down to the beach.
reason_to_go: Picnic on the bluff with whale-watching views, let the kids loose on the playground, or take the stairs to the sand at golden hour.
address: Shoreline Dr & Santa Rosa Pl, Santa Barbara, CA 93109
photo_query: Shoreline Park Santa Barbara Mesa
local_note: Open 7am–7pm; clean restrooms, ample parking (busy in summer). A steep stairway leads to a dog-friendly stretch of beach.

title: Butterfly Beach
blurb: A quiet, west-facing Montecito beach below the Biltmore, beloved for sunset views and celebrity-adjacent people-watching.
reason_to_go: Catch one of the area's best sunsets from the sand, with free street parking and the Biltmore as a backdrop.
address: Channel Dr, Montecito, CA 93108
photo_query: Butterfly Beach Montecito
local_note: Free street parking along Channel Dr. Heads up: occasional natural tar on the sand can stain feet and sandals.

title: Leadbetter Beach
blurb: A sheltered, family-friendly beach beside the harbor with calm water, picnic tables, fire pits, and tide pools — a local favorite for beginners.
reason_to_go: Learn to surf or kayak in gentle waves, grill at a fire pit, then walk the path up to Shoreline Park.
address: 801 Shoreline Dr, Santa Barbara, CA 93109
photo_query: Leadbetter Beach Santa Barbara
local_note: Pay parking lot right at the sand; first-come picnic tables and fire pits. Calm, shallow water makes it great for kids and beginners.

title: Inspiration Point
blurb: A classic ~3.8-mile out-and-back from Tunnel Road, climbing past a shaded creek to a ridge with sweeping views of the city, ocean, and Channel Islands.
reason_to_go: Hike the quintessential SB trail — paved start, single-track creek crossings, then a sudden 200-degree summit view.
address: Tunnel Rd, Santa Barbara, CA 93105
photo_query: Inspiration Point trail Santa Barbara
local_note: Park along Tunnel Rd and go early — limited parking, popular trail. Stay left at every fork for the point; creek runs in winter.

title: Arroyo Burro Beach (Hendry's)
blurb: The Mesa's beloved county beach — locals call it Hendry's — with driftwood, tide pools, a dog-friendly stretch, and a self-serve dog wash.
reason_to_go: Walk the wide sandy beach, let the dog off-leash on the west end, and climb the bluff stairs for the overlook.
address: 2981 Cliff Dr, Santa Barbara, CA 93109
photo_query: Arroyo Burro Beach Hendrys Santa Barbara
local_note: Free parking (tight in summer); restrooms, showers, and a self-service dog wash. The west end is the off-leash dog stretch.

title: East Beach
blurb: A long, clean sweep of sand east of the wharf with volleyball courts, a bike path, and lifeguards — calmer than the harbor side.
reason_to_go: Play beach volleyball, bike the waterfront path, or just stretch out on the wide, well-kept sand.
address: E Cabrillo Blvd, Santa Barbara, CA 93101
photo_query: East Beach Santa Barbara
local_note: Volleyball courts and lifeguard stations along the sand; metered beach lots nearby. The zoo and bird refuge are a few blocks east.

title: Lizard's Mouth
blurb: A sandstone playground high on West Camino Cielo, full of caves, boulders, and grippy rock formations with panoramic views over Goleta and the islands.
reason_to_go: Scramble and boulder among surreal rock formations, then watch the sunset over the Channel from the ridge.
address: W Camino Cielo, Santa Barbara, CA 93105
photo_query: Lizards Mouth Rock Santa Barbara
local_note: Trailhead is hard to spot from the road — keep driving up West Camino Cielo and watch for roadside pullouts on the left. Up Hwy 154, ~30 min from downtown.

title: Chumash Painted Cave State Historic Park
blurb: A small sandstone cave on Painted Cave Road preserving vivid Chumash rock art — colorful pictographs viewable through a protective grate.
reason_to_go: See centuries-old Chumash pictographs up close in a quiet foothill setting, paired with a scenic drive up Hwy 154.
address: Painted Cave Rd, Santa Barbara, CA 93105
photo_query: Chumash Painted Cave Santa Barbara
local_note: The access road is narrow and steep with parking for only 2–3 cars; the cave is a short rock-stair climb. View through the grate — entry not permitted.

title: Elings Park
blurb: A 230-acre privately funded park above the Mesa with hiking and mountain-bike trails, sports fields, disc golf, and hang-gliding launches.
reason_to_go: Hike or bike the hilltop trails for Channel Islands views, then catch hang-gliders launching off the ridge.
address: 1298 Las Positas Rd, Santa Barbara, CA 93105
photo_query: Elings Park Santa Barbara
local_note: Weekend entry may carry a cash fee. Trails range easy to challenging; also home to summer events on the outdoor stage.

title: SB Biergarten
blurb: An easygoing downtown beer garden at 11 Anacapa with a big patio, rotating taps, and weekly community nights.
reason_to_go: Drop in for Wednesday trivia (6-7pm, free, for a good cause) with good food and good drinks on the patio.
address: 11 Anacapa Street, Santa Barbara, CA 93101
photo_query: SB Biergarten Santa Barbara
local_note: Weekly trivia hosted by Boone Graphics benefits Central Coast nonprofits. Family-friendly early evening.

title: Sunday Polo: Robert Skene Trophy (July 12)
blurb: High-goal Sunday polo at the historic Carpinteria club — the Robert Skene Trophy opens the 16-goal series, set between the foothills and the sea.
address: Santa Barbara Polo & Racquet Club, 3375 Foothill Rd, Carpinteria, CA 93013
photo_query: Santa Barbara Polo Racquet Club Carpinteria
local_note: Gates well before the 3:30 throw-in; bring a hat, mind the dress code, and stay for the divot stomp at halftime. Tickets via the club.

title: Sunday Polo: Robert Skene Trophy (July 19)
blurb: Week two of the Robert Skene Trophy — another Sunday afternoon of 16-goal polo at the Santa Barbara Polo & Racquet Club in Carpinteria.
address: Santa Barbara Polo & Racquet Club, 3375 Foothill Rd, Carpinteria, CA 93013
photo_query: Santa Barbara Polo Racquet Club Carpinteria
local_note: Throw-in at 3:30; tailgates, terrace tables, and cabanas available. About 12 miles down the 101 from downtown.

title: Semana Nautica 5K & 2K Trail Run
blurb: A scenic Fourth-of-July trail run through Elings Park, part of Santa Barbara's long-running Semana Nautica sports festival, with a kids 2K too.
address: Elings Park, 1298 Las Positas Rd, Santa Barbara, CA 93105
photo_query: Elings Park Santa Barbara trail run
local_note: 5K starts 8:30am, kids/adult 2K at 9:30am, on Elings Park's hilltop trails. Benefits the Santa Barbara Running Association.

title: Goleta Beach Half Marathon, 10K & 5K
blurb: A flat, fast morning race along the Goleta coast — half marathon, 10K, 5K, and a kids run — starting and finishing at Goleta Beach Park.
address: Goleta Beach County Park, 5990 Sandspit Rd, Goleta, CA 93117
photo_query: Goleta Beach County Park race
local_note: An 8am start at Goleta Beach with ocean views the whole way; multiple distances plus a kids run. Confirm exact wave times on the race site.

title: Nite Moves: Summer Sunset Series
blurb: Santa Barbara's twilight tradition — a Wednesday-evening 5K run, ocean swim, or full aquathon at Leadbetter Beach, capped by a beer garden and live music.
address: Leadbetter Beach Park, 801 Shoreline Dr, Santa Barbara, CA 93101
photo_query: Nite Moves Leadbetter Beach Santa Barbara
local_note: Every Wednesday May–August. Onsite registration 5:15pm; swim 6:25pm, run 6:35pm. Beer garden, live music, kids' sand sprint, McConnell's ice cream.

title: Camino Real Marketplace Farmers' Market
blurb: Goleta's Sunday-morning certified farmers market at the Camino Real Marketplace — local produce, flowers, and prepared foods, with easy parking.
reason_to_go: Shop a relaxed Sunday market for Goleta-grown produce and treats, with the Marketplace's shops and food right there.
address: 7004 Marketplace Dr, Goleta, CA 93117
photo_query: Camino Real Marketplace farmers market Goleta
local_note: Year-round Sundays, 10am–2pm. Two hours free underground parking at the Marketplace. Accepts CalFresh EBT, WIC, and cards.

title: Goleta Beach Park
blurb: A calm, family-friendly county beach with a long fishing pier, grassy picnic lawns, and mountain-and-ocean views — quieter than the city beaches.
reason_to_go: Walk the pier at sunrise, picnic on the lawn, or watch pelicans dive — an easy, uncrowded beach day near UCSB.
address: 5986 Sandspit Rd, Goleta, CA 93117
photo_query: Goleta Beach Park pier
local_note: Lots of parking; grassy picnic areas and a long pier. A short walk from UCSB and the Obern bike path.

title: Draughtsmen Aleworks
blurb: A down-to-earth Goleta brewery with excellent draft and non-alcoholic options, a heated patio, food trucks, and a packed weekly events calendar.
reason_to_go: Try a flight from a wholesome local brewer, with Thai from the food truck and trivia, bingo, or yoga depending on the night.
address: 53 Santa Felicia Dr, Goleta, CA 93117
photo_query: Draughtsmen Aleworks Goleta
local_note: Dog-friendly with a heated patio and rotating food trucks. Weekly events incl. trivia (Tuesdays) and a Sunday cyclists group ride — confirm times.

title: M Special Brewing Company
blurb: A lively Goleta taproom with a big indoor-outdoor space, cornhole, food trucks, and a wide range of house brews.
reason_to_go: Grab a flight and a patio table with friends, with cornhole and a food truck on hand.
address: 6860 Cortona Dr Building C, Goleta, CA 93117
photo_query: M Special Brewing Goleta
local_note: Plenty of indoor and outdoor seating; dog-friendly with ample parking. Dine-in beer orders may require a food order.

title: Captain Fatty's Brewery
blurb: A warm, family-run Goleta brewery with standout beers, weekend wood-fired pizza, arcade games, and frequent live music.
reason_to_go: Pair a flight with Santa Barbara Woodfire pizza on weekends, with games and live music for an easy evening out.
address: 6489 Calle Real Ste D, Goleta, CA 93117
photo_query: Captain Fattys Brewery Goleta
local_note: Family- and dog-friendly with arcade games; wood-fired pizza Friday–Sunday and frequent live bands.

title: Old Town Coffee
blurb: A characterful Old Town Goleta coffee shop with indoor and patio seating, an arcade corner, sandwiches, and vegan options.
reason_to_go: Settle in with a coconut latte and a fresh bagel — a roomy, outlet-friendly spot to work or linger in Old Town.
address: 5877 Hollister Ave, Goleta, CA 93117
photo_query: Old Town Coffee Goleta Hollister
local_note: Lots of space, power outlets, and a patio; popular with UCSB students and remote workers. Limited street parking.

title: Los Altos Restaurant
blurb: A colorful, welcoming Old Town Goleta Mexican spot with homemade tortillas, a self-serve salsa bar, and generous plates.
reason_to_go: Dig into homemade tortillas and barbacoa tacos with nine self-serve salsas, plus a solid weekday happy hour.
address: 5892 Hollister Ave, Goleta, CA 93117
photo_query: Los Altos Restaurant Goleta
local_note: High ceilings, free parking in back, and a self-serve salsa bar. Reviewers cite a weekday happy hour — confirm exact hours before publish.

title: The Stow House
blurb: An 1872 Victorian farmhouse on a leafy Goleta estate, with period rooms, an arboretum, and the railroad depot museum next door.
reason_to_go: Step into 1870s Goleta on a weekend tour of the historic house and grounds — kid-friendly, with seasonal festivals.
address: 304 N Los Carneros Rd, Goleta, CA 93117
photo_query: Stow House Goleta historic
local_note: Open Sat 1–4pm and Sun 11am–4pm; contact ahead to confirm tours. Next to the Goleta Depot railroad museum and Lake Los Carneros trails.

title: 1st Thursday Art Walk
blurb: A free, City-sponsored evening of art and culture downtown — galleries, museums, and venues open their doors for openings, live music, receptions, and hands-on activities.
reason_to_go: Gallery-hop downtown on the first Thursday of the month — free art openings, live music, and wine, all in one walkable evening.
address: Downtown State Street corridor, Santa Barbara, CA 93101
photo_query: Santa Barbara 1st Thursday art walk downtown
local_note: First Thursday of EACH MONTH, 5–8pm (monthly cadence; schedule row uses Thursday as the weekday anchor). Free, all ages; participating galleries and venues across downtown.

title: Funk Zone Art Walk
blurb: The Funk Zone's recurring art walk — studios, galleries, and art-centric venues open from 5–8pm with exhibition openings, artist receptions, pop-up booths, live music, and tastings.
reason_to_go: Wander the Funk Zone's studios and galleries on a Friday-evening art walk, with pop-up artists, live music, and wine throughout the Zone.
address: Funk Zone (Anacapa & Yanonali Streets area), Santa Barbara, CA 93101
photo_query: Funk Zone Art Walk Santa Barbara
local_note: Bi-monthly (~every 8 weeks) on a FRIDAY evening, 5–8pm — NOT weekly; confirm the specific date on funkzone.net before publishing. Free, all ages. Often paired with food trucks and live musicians.

title: Chaucer's Books
blurb: A beloved, fiercely independent bookstore in Loreto Plaza — packed floor-to-ceiling with a deep, owner-curated selection and a big children's annex.
reason_to_go: Lose an afternoon browsing one of the last great full-service independent bookstores on the South Coast.
address: 3321 State St, Santa Barbara, CA 93105
photo_query: Chaucers Books Santa Barbara
local_note: In Loreto Plaza on upper State; knowledgeable staff and a large separate kids' section. Open late most nights.

title: The Book Den
blurb: One of the oldest bookstores in the West — a single-level downtown shop mixing new and used titles, with a standout sci-fi section.
reason_to_go: Browse new and used books side by side at a downtown institution just off State Street.
address: 15 E Anapamu St, Santa Barbara, CA 93101
photo_query: The Book Den Santa Barbara
local_note: A straightforward one-level layout with a used-book section; right by the library and museum.

title: Paradise Found
blurb: A welcoming downtown bookshop-and-gift store blending a thoughtful book selection with crystals, tarot, incense, candles, and jewelry.
reason_to_go: Browse well-curated books and metaphysical gifts — crystals, tarot, incense — in a calm, friendly space.
address: 17 E Anapamu St, Santa Barbara, CA 93101
photo_query: Paradise Found Santa Barbara
local_note: Next door to The Book Den; staff offer readings and help with crystal selection. Open daily 11–6.

title: Santa Barbara Public Market
blurb: A lively downtown food hall gathering local vendors — Thai, Korean, tacos, a wine bar, bakery, and more — under one roof near the arts district.
reason_to_go: Graze across a dozen local food and drink vendors in a casual, buzzy hall — easy for groups who can't agree.
address: 38 W Victoria St, Santa Barbara, CA 93101
photo_query: Santa Barbara Public Market food hall
local_note: Buzzy and can get loud at peak times; portions vary by vendor. Open late Thursday–Saturday.

title: Old Mission Santa Barbara
blurb: The "Queen of the Missions" — a 1786 Spanish mission on a hilltop with twin bell towers, a museum, gardens, and a historic cemetery, overlooking the city and sea.
reason_to_go: Tour the landmark mission, museum, and gardens to understand Santa Barbara's Spanish and Chumash history, with hilltop ocean views.
address: 2201 Laguna St, Santa Barbara, CA 93105
photo_query: Old Mission Santa Barbara
local_note: Open 9:30–4 daily; self-guided tour (~$17 adults) with QR-code stops. Avoid Mass times to see the church. A lawn out front is great for a picnic.

title: Santa Barbara Museum of Art
blurb: A compact, well-regarded downtown art museum on State Street with strong European, American, and Asian collections — small enough to enjoy in a focused visit.
reason_to_go: See Monet, Degas, and ukiyo-e prints in a walkable downtown museum, free on Second Sundays.
address: 1130 State St, Santa Barbara, CA 93101
photo_query: Santa Barbara Museum of Art
local_note: Open Tue–Sun 11–5 (closed Mondays). Free admission every Second Sunday, sometimes with live music.

title: Santa Barbara County Courthouse
blurb: A 1929 Spanish-Colonial Revival masterpiece — hand-painted ceilings, tiled halls, sunken gardens, and a clock tower with the best free view in town.
reason_to_go: Climb the clock tower for a free panoramic view, then wander the murals, tiles, and sunken garden of this architectural landmark.
address: 1100 Anacapa St, Santa Barbara, CA 93101
photo_query: Santa Barbara County Courthouse
local_note: Free to enter and climb the clock tower; free guided tours run twice daily. The garden and Sunken Garden host events. Open weekdays; check weekend access.

title: Harry's Plaza Cafe
blurb: A timeless Loreto Plaza institution on upper State — red-leather booths, old Santa Barbara photos, hearty American fare, and famously generous cocktails.
reason_to_go: Settle into a vintage booth for a stiff martini and classic comfort food at a true local's haunt.
address: 3313 State St, Santa Barbara, CA 93105
photo_query: Harrys Plaza Cafe Santa Barbara
local_note: In Loreto Plaza next to Chaucer's Books; known for strong drinks and a loyal regular crowd. Open late.
